label: short
chain_of_thought:
Okay, so Turbo the snail is playing this game on a board with 2024 rows and 2023 columns. There are monsters hidden in 2022 cells. The setup is such that each row except the first and last has exactly one monster, and each column has at most one monster. Turbo needs to get from the first row to the last row, but if he steps on a monster, he gets sent back to the first row. He remembers where he's been and whether there were monsters there. The question is asking for the minimum number of attempts n such that Turbo can guarantee reaching the last row by the nth attempt, no matter where the monsters are.

And there is one column with no monster at all.

The goal is to find the minimal n such that Turbo has a strategy to guarantee reaching the last row on the nth attempt or earlier, regardless of monster placements.

So, Turbo needs to navigate from R1 to R2024, avoiding the monsters in rows 2 to 2023. Since there is one column without any monsters, if Turbo can find that column, he can go straight down that column from R1 to R2024 without encountering any monsters. But how can he figure out which column is safe? Since there are 2023 columns, and only one is safe. But each column has at most one monster (in rows R2 to R2023). Therefore, each column either has a monster in one of the middle rows or is completely safe.

Turbo's challenge is to identify the safe column with as few attempts as possible.
